steps.title: Factor the second fraction's denominator and simplify
steps.content:
The denominator of the second fraction is $x^2y - 10xy$. Factor out $xy$:
$$x^2y - 10xy = xy(x - 10)$$
Now the second fraction becomes:
$$\frac{y(x-10)}{xy(x-10)} = \frac{1}{x}$$
The $(x-10)$ terms cancel. The original expression simplifies to:
$$\frac{y+13}{x-10}+\frac{1}{x}$$

steps.title: Add the fractions using common denominator
steps.content:
The common denominator is $x(x-10)$. Rewrite both fractions:
$$\frac{x(y+13)}{x(x-10)}+\frac{x-10}{x(x-10)}$$
Combine the numerators:
$$\frac{x(y+13) + (x-10)}{x(x-10)} = \frac{xy + 13x + x - 10}{x(x-10)}$$
Simplify:
$$\frac{xy + 14x - 10}{x(x-10)}$$

steps.title: Rewrite to match answer choice format
steps.content:
To match the denominators in the choices, multiply both numerator and denominator by $y$:
$$\frac{y(xy + 14x - 10)}{y \cdot x(x-10)}$$
Expand the numerator:
$$y(xy + 14x - 10) = xy^2 + 14xy - 10y$$
Expand the denominator:
$$y \cdot x(x-10) = xy(x-10) = x^2y - 10xy$$
This gives us:
$$\frac{xy^{2}+14xy-10y}{x^{2}y-10xy}$$
That matches choice C — C is correct.
